_comment: CANONICAL org ruleset — PAVED-PATH CORE profile. Applies to the framework repos (tc-pipelines, tc-fitness) via an ORGANIZATION ruleset whose conditions.repository_name includes them. These repos define the gate everyone else consumes, so EVERY PR holds for n+1 HUMAN approval (a framework regression is fleet-wide). See governance/CANONICAL-ORG-RULESET.md.
_comment_review: required_approving_review_count = 1 + require_code_owner_review = true: framework changes are never lights-out; the author cannot self-approve.
_comment_required_checks: Steady-state required set (Sonar DROPPED per Dan 2026-07-12): the three org-wide contexts ('Quality gate', 'require-work-item', 'no-attribution') PLUS the two paved-path-only contexts the consumers do not emit — 'Independent verifier' (independent second-opinion on framework changes) and 'Mutation' (mutation-survival on the engine's own tests), RESTORED per the adversarial review because a framework repo must not ship an un-mutation-tested or un-independently-verified change. NOTE 2026-07-12: Independent verifier + Mutation temporarily DROPPED — tc-fitness does not yet wire mutation-gate/independent-verifier workflows (tc-pipelines does). Wire them in tc-fitness, then re-add to this required set.
_comment_bypass: bypass_actors EMPTY. enforce_admins:false leaves the owner break-glass only.
name: org-main-core
